patterns.note: Subagents fabricate or skip validation scores when delegation prompts prime expected values or combine producer/validator roles
Incorrect task state transitions: abort marked as completed, decomposed parents marked completed before subtasks done
Assumptions about paths or config values without verification - worktree paths, terminal width, abort semantics
User questions arriving mid-operation via system-reminders are ignored instead of being acknowledged immediately
Main agent uses Write/Edit tools directly instead of delegating to subagents for implementation work
Using fabricated placeholder data instead of real values from context when testing
Using docs: commit type for Claude-facing files instead of config:. Recurs despite M255 prevention adding guidance to CLAUDE.md.
Claude manually constructs progress banners, work boxes, checkpoint boxes instead of using pre-rendered scripts. Results in misaligned boxes due to LLM inability to count display widths.
Approval gates shown without /cat:render-diff, user sees raw git diff or summary instead of 4-column table format.
compare-docs executed with single comparison run instead of mandatory two-run protocol. Completion bias causes skip of 'repeat' steps.
RCA adds validation layers to detect symptoms instead of fixing source prompts that caused subagent misbehavior.
Delegation prompts contain example values that prime subagents to fabricate or deprioritize actual execution.
Confusion about delegation mechanics: Task vs TaskCreate, manual spawning vs /cat:delegate, polling vs blocking. Subagent prompts also omit critical requirements.
Chain failures when batch operations require nested skill invocations. Subagents fail/fabricate, failure messages prime bypass, main agent does work manually.
Bash tool inline operators (!=, //, compound &&/||) get shell-escaped or produce incorrect results. Skill-level prevention insufficient.

Git squash operations produce incorrect results - orphaned backup branches, unsquashed commits at approval gate, working tree not updated after branch pointer move. RemindGitSquash hook blocks direct reset alternatives but does not enforce pre-gate squash verification. GitSquash.java now deletes backup branch on success. Squash gate added to work-with-issue Step 7.
Agent bypasses approval gate when trust=medium. Merges without formal AskUserQuestion, or interprets conversational 'yes' as approval instead of requiring explicit option selection.

Stakeholder reviewers fail to detect behavioral gaps, missing validation, and code quality issues. Reviewers read wrong branch or miss patterns spanning multiple files. Requirements stakeholder skips task-local Success Criteria for porting tasks, reading only formal REQ-IDs and missing behavioral parity checks. Pattern persists across 8 total occurrences despite multiple preventions.

Agents write lines exceeding 120-character limit in both Java source files and Markdown skill files. Prevention rules exist in java.md and common.md but agents do not actively verify line length when editing content-focused files. Formatting attention is consumed by content correctness.

Agents ask user permission before mandatory workflow steps or skip them entirely. Examples: asking permission for stakeholder review (mandatory in work-with-issue), skipping skill-builder review before approval gate. Agents cannot distinguish mandatory from optional workflow steps when skill documentation does not explicitly mark steps as MANDATORY.

Agent invokes a skill that produces interactive wizard output (AskUserQuestion) but then re-renders the output as markdown prose or responds conversationally instead of letting the skill's wizard drive the interaction. Specific to skills like /cat:config that use AskUserQuestion.

Agent fails to handle rejected or partial workflow states correctly: (1) proceeds to next phase when verify returns PARTIAL, (2) asks user 'what should I do?' after rejected stakeholder review instead of entering auto-fix loop, (3) skips re-presenting approval gate after user rejects it mid-workflow. Agent treats rejections as terminal instead of triggering the prescribed remediation.

AutoLearnMistakes hook triggers false positives on benign content: documentation severity tables matching CRITICAL ISSUE regex, embedded JSON strings in session-analyzer output containing build_failure/test_failure keywords.

Embedded action suggestions in error messages and stack traces prime agents to take action (e.g., run /cat:cleanup) when the prescribed response is STOP. Cognitive anchor from error output text overrides explicit skill-level STOP instructions. Cause signature: compliance_failure:doc_missing:issue_workflow.
Code bugs in issue workflow: lock leaks on early exit (M543) and bare sub-issue name resolution failure (M546)
Agent spawns parallel waves sequentially (one Task call per response) instead of all simultaneously in a single response
Skills missing mandatory prerequisite steps: collect-results-agent before confirm (M549), squash before merge (M559)
Exception messages omit file path and repair guidance, making self-diagnosis impossible (raw JacksonException without file name)
BFG --delete-files matches by filename only (not path); agent used it to remove root-level files, deleting ALL same-named files throughout history

action_items.description: Enforce validation separation: subagents that PRODUCE output must NOT validate it. Add explicit check in subagent-delegation.md and fail-fast in work.md when subagent claims validation without tool evidence

escalation.reason: 5 post-fix mistakes (M346, M349, M356 in current period) - documentation-level prevention ineffective
escalation.analysis: R022 update: No new PATTERN-001 recurrences in current period (M377-M391). Prior period had 5 post-fix failures (M346, M349, M356, M364, M368). A012 (prompt priming prevention) may be taking effect - descriptive placeholders instead of example values appears to reduce fabrication rate. PATTERN-020 (mandatory step bypass) shows subagent compliance gap persists in different form. Escalation remains open: if PATTERN-001 recurs, implement PostToolUse hook to detect validation claims without corresponding skill invocation evidence in session transcript.
escalation.proposed_solution: Continue monitoring. A012 (prompt priming prevention) may be stabilizing the pattern. R022: No new recurrences in current period. If recurrence in next period, implement structural enforcement: PostToolUse hook requiring tool output evidence in subagent validation claims. Current escalation level: hook (proposed but not implemented). Monitor for 2+ clean periods before closing.

action_items.description: Add hook-level validation for task status transitions: abort restores to pending, decomposed parents stay pending until all subtasks complete
Document path and config verification requirements: always check worktree context, read cat-config.json before using values, clarify abort semantics
action_items.implementation: Added 'Path and Config Verification (A003)' section to agent-architecture.md with worktree path handling, config resolution strategy, and terminology disambiguation guidelines

escalation.reason: Documentation-level prevention ineffective (4 post-fix mistakes vs 3 before)
escalation.implementation: Enhanced warn-base-branch-edit.sh to detect and warn when Edit/Write uses absolute /workspace/ paths while in a worktree, bypassing isolation
escalation.analysis: R022 update: M390 (learn skill committed retrospective metadata to main workspace instead of active worktree) adds new path/commit-routing assumption instance. warn-base-branch-edit.sh hook covers Edit/Write tool calls in worktrees, but learn skill commit routing decisions are not enforced at hook level. A022 (phase-record.md single-commit-rule) partially addresses M390 scenario. Documentation-level A003 (agent-architecture.md section) confirmed ineffective across 5+ instances. Hook enforcement covers only a subset of path assumption failure modes.
escalation.proposed_solution: Expand hook coverage to detect commits to main workspace while active worktree exists. A022 prevention (phase-record.md) addresses the specific M390 learn-skill scenario. Consider adding commit destination validation to learn skill phase-record subagent. R022: Escalation remains open; M390 prevention implemented but not yet verified.

action_items.description: Strengthen user input handling: clarify that current task means current tool call not entire workflow. Add explicit instruction to respond in very next message when system-reminder contains user question.
action_items.implementation: Updated inject-session-instructions.sh to clarify that IMPORTANT After completing your current task means after current tool call, not entire workflow

action_items.description: Enforce main agent delegation boundary: main agent NEVER uses Write/Edit tools on source files during task execution. All implementation work must be delegated to subagents.
action_items.implementation: Hook warn-base-branch-edit.sh already warns about main agent source edits (A003/M097/M302). Emits warning when Edit/Write targets non-orchestration files. Warning includes delegation guidance.

action_items.description: Prevent test data fabrication: when testing commands/scripts, always use real values from context (e.g., session ID from SessionStart) instead of placeholder/fabricated data.
action_items.implementation: Added anti-pattern M300 to work.md documenting that session IDs must come from SessionStart context, not fabricated placeholders. Script validates UUID format and fails on invalid input.

action_items.description: Add hook-level validation for commit type selection: detect when docs: is used for plugin/ or CLAUDE.md paths and suggest config: instead
action_items.implementation: Added PreToolUse check in validate_commit_type.py that blocks docs: commits when staged files match Claude-facing patterns (plugin/, CLAUDE.md, .claude/, hooks/, skills/, concepts/, commands/). Also added plugin/ to PostToolUse verify_commit_type.py for defense-in-depth.

action_items.description: Enforce pre-rendered output usage: add PostToolUse hook to detect manual box/banner construction patterns and warn agent. Pattern detection: box-drawing chars in agent output not preceded by script execution.
action_items.implementation: Created detect_manual_boxes.py PostToolUse handler that monitors conversation log for box-drawing characters in assistant messages without preceding script execution. Warns with A008/PATTERN-008 context.

escalation.reason: 4 post-fix mistakes (M331, M332, M333, M334) - hook detects but does not prevent bypass
escalation.analysis: Current PostToolUse hook warns AFTER manual construction occurs. Need PreToolUse enforcement that BLOCKS text generation containing box-drawing characters unless preceded by script call.
escalation.proposed_solution: Add PreToolUse blocking hook that requires script invocation before any response containing box-drawing chars. Alternative: Move all box/banner generation to mandatory skill invocations.
escalation.implementation: Added FAIL-FAST instructions to work.md, work-with-task.md, status.md. When preprocessing fails, agents must run scripts manually - never construct boxes.

action_items.description: Enforce render-diff at approval gates: add PreToolUse hook on AskUserQuestion that checks for approval keywords and validates render-diff was invoked.
action_items.implementation: Already implemented via warn-approval-without-renderdiff.sh PreToolUse hook. Detects approval-related AskUserQuestion calls and warns if render-diff.py wasn't invoked recently.

action_items.description: Restructure compare-docs to make two-run validation structurally impossible to skip: Step 1 spawns all 4 extraction agents, Step 2 spawns both comparison agents. No 'repeat' instruction that completion bias can skip.
action_items.implementation: M354 prevention already restructured compare-docs to spawn all agents upfront. M357 added explicit batch handling. Two-run is now default structure.

action_items.description: When subagent produces wrong output, fix the SOURCE (prompt/skill) not add validation layers. learn-from-mistakes skill must require asking 'Why did subagent do X?' and fixing prompt if answer involves prompt content.
action_items.implementation: M355 prevention added source-vs-symptom guidance to learn skill. M358 added explicit warning against independent validation layers.

action_items.description: Use descriptive placeholders in delegation prompt examples (e.g., '<actual score from /compare-docs>') instead of example values like '1.0'. Require per-file score reporting with evidence of skill invocation.
action_items.implementation: M346 prevention added descriptive placeholder guidance and per-file reporting requirement. M350 prevention restructured prompts with critical requirements first.

action_items.description: Consolidate delegation guidance: (1) Document Task vs TaskCreate distinction clearly, (2) Prefer /cat:delegate for batch/parallel work, (3) Always use blocking TaskOutput calls, (4) Include CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS checklist in delegation prompts
action_items.implementation: Already implemented: Task vs TaskCreate in subagent-delegation.md (M372), /cat:delegate examples in delegate/SKILL.md, blocking TaskOutput guidance in lines 901-957 (M293/M373), SUBAGENT-PROMPT-CHECKLIST.md for CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

action_items.description: Strengthen hook patterns: (1) Account for git -C flag in worktree hooks, (2) Test hook patterns against command variations, (3) Add hook coverage tests
action_items.implementation_note: Implemented in 2.1-strengthen-hook-patterns: Updated validate-worktree-branch.sh and block_main_rebase.py to handle git -C flag variations, added test_git_flag_patterns.py with 12 test cases

action_items.description: Failure messages must include actionable recovery guidance: retry with single file, escalate to user, NEVER bypass skill
action_items.implementation: M429 prevention added to error-handling.md: explicit guidance for main agent when subagent fails at skill invocation

action_items.description: Remove detailed failure response examples from skill docs to prevent fabrication
action_items.implementation: Removed detailed failure JSON examples from work-batch-executor/SKILL.md that were being copied as fabricated responses

action_items.description: Enforce Python for all comparison/conditional logic in bash handlers. Add detection for !=, ==, // operators in Bash commands. Current skill-level prevention (M431) insufficient.
action_items.implementation: Root cause was incorrect quoting, not a bash limitation. jq filters in double quotes expose != to zsh history expansion; single quotes prevent this. Variable interpolation should use jq --arg instead of shell interpolation. No hook needed — the fix is proper quoting discipline, not banning bash comparisons. No recurrences since 2026-02-06.

action_items.description: Fix squash workflow to require rebase-before-squash and clarify STATE.md grouping with implementation files, not as separate config commit.
action_items.implementation: Three code fixes verified as implemented: (1) GitSquash.java line 190 deletes backup branch on success. (2) MergeAndCleanup.java uses git merge --ff-only which atomically updates ref, index, and working tree. (3) work-with-issue delegates squash to work-squash subagent which consolidates all commits. Additionally: delegate-squash-to-subagent (closed 2026-02-25), cleanup-squash-backups-in-merge (closed 2026-02-20), fix-squash-workflow (closed), delegate-squash-to-subagent (closed) all address sub-issues.

escalation.reason: Squash workflow continues to fail despite A018. 4 post-fix mistakes (M484, M488, M500, M503). M503 added RemindGitSquash hook blocking git reset --soft, but underlying issue is worktree staleness and manual squash bypass.
escalation.analysis: 2026-03-03 update: All three proposed code fixes verified as implemented in codebase. GitSquash.java deletes backup on success (line 190). MergeAndCleanup uses git merge --ff-only (atomically syncs working tree). work-with-issue delegates to work-squash subagent. Escalation closed pending effectiveness measurement.

action_items.description: Add fallback banner script re-invocation when pre-rendered banners unavailable due to context compaction. Skills should include script path for manual re-invocation.
action_items.implementation: M452 prevention updated work-with-issue skill to include explicit progress-banner script commands at every phase step, eliminating dependency on pre-rendered context.

action_items.description: Enforce approval gate: PreToolUse hook on Task(work-merge) must verify AskUserQuestion with 'Approve and merge' was selected when trust != high. Conversational responses are NOT approval.
action_items.implementation: M489 prevention added Approval Gate Protocol to .claude/rules/hooks.md. M480 added PreToolUse hook blocking work-merge spawn without explicit approval. Both already committed.

escalation.reason: 6 post-fix failures. Hook validates AskUserQuestion was called but does not check that answers object is non-empty. M459/M462/M463/M464 all bypass approval gate via empty answers {}.
escalation.analysis: R026 escalation: 6 post-fix failures recorded. However, issue 2.1-fix-approval-gate-empty-answers-handling was closed 2026-03-04 with a documentation fix adding explicit empty-answers handling to work-with-issue Step 8. M459/M462/M463/M464 occurred before or during the fix. Fix implemented; effectiveness pending next monitoring period.

action_items.description: Require stakeholder reviewers to diff against base branch and read all modified files before reviewing. Add mandatory file-reading evidence to review format.
action_items.implementation: Implemented in v2.1-require-reviewer-file-evidence (closed 2026-02-22). Added mandatory Evidence section to stakeholder reviewer JSON output and diff-analysis-first requirement to all 10 stakeholder agent definitions.

action_items.description: Fix learn skill commit-grouping: when prevention type is skill/process/documentation/config and no active worktree exists, include prevention files in same commit as retrospective metadata. Only split commits when prevention targets a worktree (code_fix, hook, validation).
action_items.implementation: M366 prevention added single-commit-rule to phase-record.md Step 12-13: when prevention is skill/process/documentation/config type, group prevention file and retrospective metadata into one commit in Step 13. Only split when prevention targets a worktree.

action_items.description: Add active line-length verification reminder to phase-prevent.md: after editing any Markdown or Java file, verify all added lines comply with the 120-character limit (common.md). Include in phase-prevent Step 4 a checkstep: count characters in each added line and wrap any that exceed 120.
action_items.implementation: Implemented in v2.1-add-line-length-verification-reminder (closed 2026-03-01). Added line-length check-step to phase-prevent.md Step 4.

action_items.description: Explicitly label all mandatory workflow steps as MANDATORY in work-with-issue and other orchestration skills. Add explicit instruction that mandatory steps do not require user permission and must not be skipped. Affected steps: stakeholder-review (Step 5), skill-builder review before approval gate, squash before approval gate (Step 6). Add a MANDATORY STEPS checklist at the top of work-with-issue/first-use.md.
action_items.implementation: Implemented in v2.1-label-mandatory-workflow-steps (closed 2026-03-02). Added MANDATORY STEPS checklist to work-with-issue/first-use.md and labeled stakeholder-review, skill-builder review, and squash steps as MANDATORY.

action_items.description: Document skill output forwarding protocol: when a skill produces AskUserQuestion wizard output, the invoking agent must call AskUserQuestion directly (not render it as prose). Add to wizard-based skills (config, etc.) explicit note: 'After invoking this skill, forward the AskUserQuestion tool call verbatim — do not respond conversationally.'
action_items.implementation: Implemented in v2.1-document-skill-output-forwarding-protocol (closed 2026-03-01). Added forwarding note to wizard-based skills instructing invoking agents to forward AskUserQuestion calls verbatim.

action_items.description: Add explicit rejection-state handling to work-with-issue: (1) PARTIAL verify result = STOP, do NOT proceed to stakeholder review — fix unmet conditions first; (2) Rejected stakeholder review = automatically enter re-work loop (fix issues, re-run review) without asking user permission; (3) User rejects AskUserQuestion approval gate mid-workflow = re-present the gate in the NEXT response, do not skip it. Add 'REJECTION HANDLING' section to work-with-issue/first-use.md covering all three cases.
action_items.implementation: Implemented in v2.1-add-rejection-state-handling (closed 2026-03-01). Added REJECTION HANDLING section to work-with-issue/first-use.md covering all three rejection cases.

action_items.description: Fix AutoLearnMistakes pattern matching to exclude false positives from documentation tables, code blocks, and embedded JSON in tool output. Add context-aware filtering: only match patterns in plain text assistant messages.
action_items.implementation: Addressed by 2.1-fix-autolearn-pattern2-false-positive (closed). Removed ^(FAIL:|FAILED\s) sub-pattern from Pattern 2 in AutoLearnMistakes.java.

action_items.description: Remove or neutralize embedded action suggestions from error messages and skill error paths. Error output must not contain runnable commands or links that prime agents to act. STOP instructions must be reinforced structurally: add STOP banner immediately before error output in skill No-result handlers.

Fix lock leak in WorkPrepare.execute(): release the lock in ALL early-exit paths (OVERSIZED, CORRUPT, etc.) when a lock was acquired by IssueDiscovery.findIssueInDir(). Restructure to not acquire the lock until after token estimation passes.

Fix IssueDiscovery.allSubissuesClosed() to handle bare sub-issue names (without version prefix). When QUALIFIED_NAME_PATTERN fails to match, resolve bare names by deriving path from parentVersionDir directly. Prevent silent 'continue' when sub-issue name lacks prefix, which falsely reports all closed.

Add pre-spawn wave checklist to work-implement-agent: before issuing ANY Task call when WAVES_COUNT >= 2, agent must write ALL wave prompts as prose text in the same response, complete a numbered wave list confirming count, then issue ALL Task calls simultaneously. No Task call may appear without the checklist preceding it.

Add missing prerequisite steps to two skills: (1) work-implement-agent/first-use.md: add explicit 'Call cat:collect-results-agent BEFORE any Skill or Task call after implementation subagent completes' step. (2) git-merge-linear-agent/first-use.md: add mandatory 'Invoke cat:git-squash-agent' step before merge.

Wrap JacksonException in RecordLearning.loadOrCreateMistakesFile() with an IOException that includes the file path being parsed and instructs user to inspect/repair. Error message must name the file: 'Failed to parse mistakes file at <path>: <original message>. Inspect and fix the file, then retry.'

Extend AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() to also strip git diff lines (lines starting with +, -, or @@). This prevents deleted/added file content in diff output from triggering false-positive mistake detection. Extend the existing open issue fix-autolearn-java-source-false-positive to cover both Java source code lines AND git diff lines.

Add CRITICAL warning to git-rewrite-history-agent/first-use.md --delete-files section: (1) Warn that --delete-files matches by FILENAME ONLY across all paths, (2) Document git filter-repo --path X --invert-paths as path-specific alternative, (3) Add pre-check bash command to find all files sharing the same name before using --delete-files.

escalations.original_fix_description: Enforce validation separation: subagents that PRODUCE output must NOT validate it. Add explicit check in subagent-delegation.md and fail-fast in work.md when subagent claims validation without tool evidence

failure_analysis.expected_result: No new subagent_validation_fabrication instances after documentation fix
failure_analysis.actual_result: 5 post-fix failures (M346, M349, M356, M364, M368) confirm documentation-level prevention is insufficient
failure_analysis.root_cause: Documentation rules are advisory; agents can ignore or misinterpret them under load. No structural barrier prevents a subagent from claiming validation without tool evidence.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: Missing structural enforcement: no hook or tool-level check verifies that validation claims are backed by actual skill invocation evidence in the session transcript

proposed_solution.description: Implement PostToolUse hook that detects when a subagent returns a validation score/verdict without a corresponding skill invocation in the session transcript. Hook should fail-fast with an error if validation is claimed without evidence.
layers: PostToolUse hook: block Task completions claiming validation without tool evidence
Skill update: make work-confirm-agent explicitly emit tool-evidence markers before validation claims
Threshold: 0 new PATTERN-001 recurrences before closing escalation

escalations.original_fix_description: Document path and config verification requirements: always check worktree context, read cat-config.json before using values, clarify abort semantics

failure_analysis.expected_result: No new path_config_assumption instances after documentation + hook fix
failure_analysis.actual_result: PATTERN-003 worsening: 3 total occurrences before fix, 5 after fix. occurrences_after_fix=5 exceeds original count.
failure_analysis.root_cause: warn-base-branch-edit.sh hook covers Edit/Write absolute path calls in worktrees but misses: (1) commit routing decisions in learn skill, (2) config reads that use assumed values rather than explicit disk reads, (3) non-Edit/Write operations that assume path context.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: Hook coverage is too narrow — only catches Edit/Write tool calls with absolute /workspace/ paths. Agents still assume config values and commit destinations without verification via other code paths.

proposed_solution.description: Expand path/config enforcement to cover commit routing (learn skill phase-record) and non-Edit/Write path assumptions. Add worktree-context verification step at the start of each subagent invocation that touches files.
layers: Expand warn-base-branch-edit.sh to detect Bash tool calls containing absolute /workspace/ paths while in a worktree
Add mandatory worktree-context check at start of work-prepare, work-implement, work-review, work-merge agents
Learn skill: add commit-destination check before any git commit in phase-record subagent

escalations.original_fix_description: Enforce approval gate: PreToolUse hook on Task(work-merge) must verify AskUserQuestion with 'Approve and merge' was selected when trust != high. Conversational responses are NOT approval.

failure_analysis.expected_result: No approval gate bypasses after hook + empty-answers fix (2026-03-04)
failure_analysis.actual_result: 6 post-fix failures (M489, M446, M459, M462, M463, M464). Hook validates call occurred but not answer content. Empty answers object {} bypasses gate.
failure_analysis.root_cause: The PreToolUse hook checks that AskUserQuestion was invoked but does not validate the answers field is non-empty and contains an explicit approval. Empty {} answers are treated as passing the gate.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: Two-layer failure: (1) hook validation too weak (presence-only, not content), (2) work-with-issue Step 8 documentation fix insufficient — agent still spawns work-merge before reading answers.

proposed_solution.description: Fix the PreToolUse hook to validate answers content, not just AskUserQuestion presence. Require that answers object is non-empty AND contains a key matching the approval question with a truthy value.
layers: Hook fix: validate answers object is non-empty in PreToolUse enforcement
Hook fix: require explicit approval answer key, not just any non-empty answers
Skill fix: work-with-issue Step 8 must explicitly check answers before spawning work-merge
Regression test: add test case for empty-answers bypass scenario

escalations.original_fix_description: Enforce validation separation: subagents that PRODUCE output must NOT validate it. ESCALATE-2026-03-10-001 proposed PostToolUse hook to block Task completions claiming validation without tool evidence.

failure_analysis.expected_result: ESCALATE-2026-03-10-001 hook proposal would eliminate new PATTERN-001 recurrences
failure_analysis.actual_result: Hook was proposed but not yet implemented. Current period (M532-M542) shows PATTERN-001 IMPROVING (9 total, 5 remaining) but not resolved. Escalation-level fix has not been built.
failure_analysis.root_cause: The previous escalation correctly identified the gap (structural enforcement needed) but remained in 'proposed' state — no implementation issue was created. Proposals without implementation issues are not tracked to completion.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: No CAT issue exists to implement the PostToolUse validation-evidence hook. Escalation stalled at analysis stage.

proposed_solution.description: Create a CAT issue (2.1-validation-evidence-hook) to implement the PostToolUse hook that verifies subagent Task completions include tool-invocation evidence before accepting any validation claim. Assign high priority. Block retrospective closure until issue is created.
layers: Create issue: 2.1-validation-evidence-hook
Hook: PostToolUse on Task tool — scan completion message for validation claim keywords
Validation claim without prior skill invocation evidence fails with STOP
Test: fabricated validation vs real skill invocation

escalations.original_fix_description: Document path and config verification requirements. ESCALATE-2026-03-10-002 proposed expanding hook coverage to Bash tool calls with absolute /workspace/ paths and mandatory worktree-context checks at subagent start.

failure_analysis.expected_result: ESCALATE-2026-03-10-002 hook expansion would stop path_config_assumption recurrences
failure_analysis.actual_result: PATTERN-003 status is WORSENING — 3 occurrences before fix, 5 after fix. Current period analysis confirms it remains worsening. The hook expansion was proposed but not implemented.
failure_analysis.root_cause: Same implementation gap as ESCALATE-2026-03-10-001: proposed solutions without corresponding CAT issues are never built. Additionally, M537 (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT empty without fail-fast guard) shows a new vector: missing environment variable guards in skill bash blocks, distinct from path/config assumption in commit routing.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: Two uncovered vectors: (1) commit routing assumptions in learn skill (proposed but unimplemented), (2) missing env-var fail-fast guards in work-implement-agent/first-use.md bash blocks. Both allow agents to proceed with incorrect context silently.

proposed_solution.description: Split into two targeted fixes: (A) Add CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT fail-fast guard to work-implement-agent/first-use.md Step 2 as a skill fix (low effort, directly prevents M537 recurrence). (B) Create issue for learn skill commit-destination check. Both must be CAT issues, not proposals.
layers: Skill fix: work-implement-agent/first-use.md Step 2 — add guard: if [[ -z ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-} ]]; then ERROR + STOP
Issue: 2.1-learn-skill-commit-destination-check
Hook: expand warn-base-branch-edit.sh to detect absolute /workspace/ in Bash tool calls from worktrees

escalations.original_fix_description: Enforce approval gate: PreToolUse hook must validate answers content (non-empty, correct key). ESCALATE-2026-03-10-003 proposed hook fix plus work-with-issue Step 8 skill fix.

failure_analysis.expected_result: ESCALATE-2026-03-10-003 hook + skill fix eliminates approval gate bypass
failure_analysis.actual_result: PATTERN-017 (approval_gate_bypass) is IMPROVING (9 total, 6 remaining) but not resolved. The specific fix for answers content validation has not been implemented — the hook still only checks presence, not content.
failure_analysis.root_cause: Same pattern: escalation proposed a solution but no implementation issue was created. The fix requires changes to the PreToolUse hook Java code (ApprovalGateEnforcer.java or equivalent) plus work-with-issue.md Step 8 documentation change.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: The hook validator accepts an empty answers object {} as 'passed'. This is the primary bypass vector identified in M489, M446, M459, M462, M463, M464. Fix is well-understood but not in any open issue.

proposed_solution.description: Create issue 2.1-fix-approval-gate-answers-validation to implement: (1) Hook change — reject Task(work-merge) when answers is null or empty object, (2) Hook change — require answers to contain the approval question key with truthy value, (3) Test — add regression test for empty-answers bypass.
layers: Create issue: 2.1-fix-approval-gate-answers-validation
Hook fix: reject empty answers {} at PreToolUse gate for work-merge tasks
Hook fix: validate approval answer key presence and value
Test: empty-answers bypass regression test
Skill fix: work-with-issue Step 8 documentation update

escalations.original_fix_description: Enforce validation separation: subagents that PRODUCE output must NOT validate it. ESCALATE-2026-03-14-001 proposed creating CAT issue 2.1-validation-evidence-hook for PostToolUse hook.

failure_analysis.expected_result: CAT issue 2.1-validation-evidence-hook would be created from R029 escalation
failure_analysis.actual_result: No new PATTERN-001 recurrences in current period (M543-M564). Pattern improving but escalation-level hook still not implemented.
failure_analysis.root_cause: Escalation proposed issue creation but no evidence it was created. Proposals without follow-through remain stalled.
failure_analysis.gap_identified: PATTERN-001 shows improvement (no new recurrences this period) but structural hook enforcement is still absent. The proposed PostToolUse hook for validation-evidence verification remains unbuilt.

proposed_solution.description: PATTERN-001 has 0 new occurrences in this period. If next period also shows 0 recurrences, close this escalation as resolved by existing documentation fixes. If recurrence, implement 2.1-validation-evidence-hook immediately.
layers: Monitor for 1 more clean period (0 PATTERN-001 recurrences)
